# terrible_cisco_dnac_energy_sites_id_info (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `attribute` (String) Attribute query parameter. Supported Attributes id, siteHierarchy, siteHierarchyId, siteType, latitude, longitude, energyConsumed, estimatedCost, estimatedEmission, carbonIntensity, numberOfDevices If length of attribute list is too long, please use 'view' param instead. Examples attribute=siteHierarchy (single attribute requested) attribute=siteHierarchy&attribute=energyConsumed (multiple attributes requested).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `deviceCategory` (String) DeviceCategory query parameter. The list of device categories. Note that this filter specifies which devices will be included when calculating energy consumption values, rather than specifying the list of returned sites. Examples `deviceCategory=Switch` (single device category requested) `deviceCategory=Switch&deviceCategory=Router` (multiple device categories with comma separator).
- `endTime` (Number) EndTime query parameter. End time to which API queries the data set related to the resource. It must be specified in UNIX epochtime in milliseconds. Value is inclusive. If `endTime` is not provided, API will default to one day after `startTime`. If `startTime` is not provided either, API will default to current time.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `headers` (String) Additional headers.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `startTime` (Number) StartTime query parameter. Start time from which API queries the data set related to the resource. It must be specified in UNIX epochtime in milliseconds. Value is inclusive. If `startTime` is not provided, API will default to one day before `endTime`.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `taskId` (String) TaskId query parameter. Used to retrieve asynchronously processed & stored data. When this parameter is used, the rest of the request params will be ignored.
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `views` (String) Views query parameter. The specific summary view being requested. This is an optional parameter which can be passed to get one or more of the specific health data summaries associated with sites. ### Response data proviced by each view 1. **Site** id, siteHierarchy, siteHierarchyId, siteType, latitude, longitude 2. **Energy** energyConsumed, estimatedCost, estimatedEmission, carbonIntensity, numberOfDevices When this query parameter is not added the default summaries are **site,energy** Examples views=site (single view requested) views=site,energy (multiple views requested).

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `dnac_response` (String) A dictionary or list with the response returned by the Cisco DNAC Python SDK
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
